title: Alpaca Format
existing_short_definition: A simple (and now dated) instruction-tuning prompt format using '### Instruction:' / '### Input:' / '### Response:' headers, popularized by Stanford Alpaca in 2023.

title: Prefix Tokens / BOS (Beginning of Sequence)
existing_short_definition: Special tokens (<s>, <|begin_of_text|>, [CLS]) prepended to every sequence to mark where it starts — a sentinel the model uses to anchor attention.

title: BPE (Byte Pair Encoding)
existing_short_definition: The most common tokenization algorithm for modern LLMs — repeatedly merges the most-frequent byte pairs in the training corpus to build a vocabulary of subword units.
existing_code_example:
# Inspect tokenization with tiktoken
import tiktoken
enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4o')
tokens = enc.encode('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog')
print(len(tokens), tokens)
# Decode back to see token boundaries:
for t in tokens:
    print(repr(enc.decode([t])))

title: Byte-Level BPE
existing_short_definition: A BPE variant that operates on raw UTF-8 bytes instead of Unicode characters, giving a 256-byte starting alphabet that covers any possible text.

title: Chat Template
existing_short_definition: The exact string format a model expects for multi-turn conversations — user vs assistant roles, system prompts, special tokens separating turns.
existing_code_example:
from transformers import AutoTokenizer
tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct')
messages = [
    {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are a concise assistant.'},
    {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'},
]
prompt = tok.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True)
print(prompt)  # Shows exact formatted string

title: Chat Template (Jinja)
existing_short_definition: A Jinja-formatted template string shipped with each chat model's tokenizer that defines how to turn messages into a formatted prompt.

title: ChatML
existing_short_definition: A simple chat-message format using special tokens to mark role boundaries — originally introduced by OpenAI, now common in open-source chat templates.

title: EOS (End of Sequence) Token
existing_short_definition: A special token that tells the model to stop generating — hitting it during inference is how the model signals 'I'm done.'

title: Pad Token
existing_short_definition: A special token used to pad short sequences in a batch to a common length so they can be processed together efficiently.

title: SentencePiece
existing_short_definition: Google's open-source tokenizer library that treats the input as a raw sequence of characters (no word-boundary assumption), supporting both BPE and Unigram LM algorithms.

title: ShareGPT Format
existing_short_definition: A multi-turn conversation dataset format popularized by the ShareGPT dataset, using role-tagged message arrays.

title: Special Tokens (BOS, EOS, PAD)
existing_short_definition: Reserved token IDs a tokenizer uses to mark structure — beginning of sequence (BOS), end of sequence (EOS), padding (PAD), end-of-turn markers, etc.
existing_code_example:
tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct')
print(tok.bos_token_id, tok.eos_token_id, tok.pad_token_id)
print(tok.special_tokens_map)
# For generation:
output = model.generate(**inputs, eos_token_id=[tok.eos_token_id, tok.convert_tokens_to_ids('<|eot_id|>')])

title: Subword Tokenization
existing_short_definition: Splitting text into units smaller than words but larger than characters — the dominant tokenization family for modern LLMs.

title: Tiktoken
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's fast open-source BPE tokenizer library and the reference implementation for GPT-family models.
existing_code_example:
import tiktoken
enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4o')
print(len(enc.encode('Hello world')))

title: Token
existing_short_definition: A token is a chunk of text — roughly a word or part of a word — that AI models process as a single unit.

title: Token Budget
existing_short_definition: The total number of tokens an application allocates to a single LLM call — system prompt + messages + retrieved context + output headroom.

title: Token Cost
existing_short_definition: The dollars-per-million-tokens price charged for inputs and outputs — the unit economics of every LLM feature.

title: Token Healing
existing_short_definition: A trick at structured-generation time where the tokenizer 'heals' a partial prefix by backtracking one token, avoiding awkward splits during constrained decoding.

title: Tokenizer
existing_short_definition: The component that converts raw text into integer token IDs (and back) before and after model inference.

title: Tool Use Formatting (In-Context)
existing_short_definition: The model-specific format for representing tool definitions, tool calls, and tool results inside the prompt — typically JSON inside special tokens.

title: Unigram LM Tokenizer
existing_short_definition: A tokenization algorithm that starts with a large seed vocabulary and iteratively prunes it to maximize log-likelihood under a unigram language model.

title: Tokenizer Vocabulary Extension
existing_short_definition: Adding new tokens to an existing tokenizer — for languages, domain jargon, or special structural tokens — without retraining from scratch.

title: Vocabulary Size
existing_short_definition: The number of distinct tokens a tokenizer can emit — typically 30k–260k for modern LLMs, trading expressiveness for embedding-table size.

title: WordPiece
existing_short_definition: A subword tokenization algorithm (used by BERT) that greedily segments words into pieces that maximize training-corpus likelihood.
